name: Split Pane
description: Resizable terminal panes with keyboard and mouse controls for multi-panel layouts.

horizontal.description: Side by side panes with vertical divider

quad.description: Four-pane 2x2 grid layout with cross-shaped drag handles at the center intersection

visual_brief: Multi-panel layout with resizable dividers between panes. The horizontal preset splits the viewport into left and right panels with a vertical drag handle between them. The vertical preset splits into top and bottom with a horizontal drag handle. The quad preset creates four panels in a 2x2 grid with cross-shaped drag handles at the center. Each pane has a monospace terminal aesthetic with a dark background. Drag handles render as a thin line with a grip indicator (three dots or lines). Double-clicking a handle resets to equal sizing.

responsive.mobile: Panes stack vertically (no side-by-side splitting). Tab-based switching between panes instead of simultaneous view. No drag handles.
responsive.tablet: Horizontal split available. Drag handles have larger touch targets (24px hit area). Quad preset unavailable.
responsive.desktop: Full split pane with all presets. Keyboard shortcuts for resizing. Drag handles at standard size.

layout_hints.pane_ownership: The pattern owns pane boundaries, dividers, and resize affordances. Child pages or viewers should not add their own competing split structures unless explicitly nested by the contract.
layout_hints.focus_hierarchy: One pane should always feel primary and the others supportive. Resize handles should reinforce that relationship rather than turning the layout into an undifferentiated grid.
layout_hints.mobile_fallback: When the split collapses on mobile, preserve pane semantics through tabs or stacked sections rather than pretending simultaneous multi-pane editing is still available.

motion.micro: Resize handles may brighten or thicken slightly on hover and focus so the drag affordance is obvious.
motion.transitions: Pane resizing should feel direct and continuous. Collapsing or resetting panes should animate just enough to preserve orientation without adding theatrical motion.

keyboard: Focusable resize handles should expose arrow-key resizing in addition to pointer drag.
Pane order and active pane state should remain clear when navigating by keyboard.
announcements: Resize handles and pane labels should communicate orientation and current size changes to assistive technology when adjusted.
